subject: GRB 190608A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 00:12:18 UT on 8 Jun 2019, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 190608A (trigger 581645543.394168 / 190608009).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 225.7, Dec = -31.4 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 15h 02m, -31d 23'), with a statistical uncertainty of 2.2 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 71.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn190608009/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn190608009.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn190608009/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn190608009.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn190608009/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn190608009.gif
